title: ETS bag and duplicate_bag table types
purpose: `bag` tables allow multiple tuples with the same key but reject exact duplicates. `duplicate_bag` allows fully duplicate tuples. Both are used in OTP for multi-value indexes (e.g., process group membership, event handlers).
task: Implement `EtsBag` and `EtsDuplicateBag` as EtsTable implementations using `DashMap<Term, Vec<Term>>` (key → list of tuples).

requirements.title: EtsBag implementation
acceptance: `insert(tuple)`: adds tuple to the key's list. If exact duplicate exists, no-op (bag rejects exact duplicates)
`lookup(key)`: returns all tuples with that key
`delete_key(key)`: removes all tuples with that key
Test: insert {a,1}, insert {a,2} → lookup a returns [{a,1},{a,2}]
Test: insert {a,1} twice → lookup a returns [{a,1}] (duplicate rejected)

requirements.title: EtsDuplicateBag implementation
acceptance: `insert(tuple)`: always adds, even if exact duplicate exists
Test: insert {a,1} twice → lookup a returns [{a,1},{a,1}]

requirements.title: Wire bag types into table creation
requirements.spec: Wire bag types into table creation
acceptance: Test: create bag table, insert duplicates, verify correct semantics

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement ordered iteration — bags are unordered like sets
SHALL NOT implement match specifications — that's B-096/B-097
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
